description: Sheet-based wallet surface composed of ConnectWallet (trigger), Sheet (drawer container), and the Wallet / NetworkBadge / TokenAmount / TxStatus primitives inside. When connected, the trigger opens the drawer; when disconnected, the trigger fires onConnect and the drawer stays closed.

keyboard.action: Moves focus to the ConnectWallet trigger, then (when the drawer is open) through each focusable element inside the drawer in reading order.
Activates the connect trigger or the focused action inside the drawer (e.g. Disconnect).
Closes the open drawer and returns focus to the trigger.
Cycles focus backwards. Focus is trapped inside the drawer while open (Sheet behavior).

focus.notes: Focus trapping is delegated to Radix Dialog (which Sheet wraps). The trigger element itself is only focusable when the drawer is closed; while open, focus lives inside the drawer.

announcements: Trigger announces its connect state (same as ConnectWallet).
On open, the drawer announces its role, title, and description in reading order.
Connected-state sections (address, balance, transactions) are announced as a flat region; consider wrapping each section in a labelled heading if you need landmarks.
The Disconnect button announces "Disconnect" and fires onDisconnect on activation.

contrast.text: All labels, balance, and address text use foreground or text-muted-foreground; both meet WCAG AA on the default theme over bg-background
contrast.ui: Sheet backdrop, NetworkBadge, and separators all meet 3:1 non-text contrast on the default theme

motion.reducedMotion: Sheet's slide-in animation is driven by Radix data-state attributes. To honor prefers-reduced-motion, wire motion-reduce:animate-none on the Sheet's className in your theme layer.

consumerResponsibilities: Own the connection state machine; WalletDrawer is presentational. Derive `status`, `address`, `name`, `chainName` from your connector (wagmi / viem / etc.).
Populate the `balance` slot with a live value; WalletDrawer never fetches balances.
Populate the `transactions` slot with recent TxStatus entries or leave it undefined; the drawer renders a compact empty section when absent.
Handle errors (user rejected, wrong network) outside the drawer; show them in a toast or inline alert. The drawer's job is to present state, not diagnose it.
